title: Quick Fungal DNA extraction from colonies on plates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"65q0s","text":"Adapted from Chi, M. H., Park, S. Y., \u0026 Lee, Y. H. (2009).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":13,"length":45}],"data":[]},{"key":"e5pcq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"daj2k","text":"Colony PCR on fungal colonies grown on plates does not work as well as it does for bacteria (it usually doesn't work at all). DNA therefore needs to be extracted first. As this DNA will only be used as a PCR template to check for presence / absence of individual genes I am not too concerend about high molecular weight or purity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"jr45","text":"This protocol is quick and can be used to process several samples at the same time. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"98E84EA0ED5F4957A8F1CBE068BA0D66","url":"https://doi.org/10.5423/PPJ.2009.25.1.108"}}}}
steps: %s %s 1M TrisHCl pH 8.0 100 ml 12.14 g Tris base or Trizma base up to 100 ml deionised H2O Adjust pH concentrated HCl %s %s 2M KCl (Potassium chloride) 200 ml 15.11 g KCl (Potassium chloride) up to 200 ml deionised H2O A B 1 500 mM NaEDTA (pH 8.0) 100 ml 2 18.6 g EDTA disodium salt dihydrate 3 up to 100 ml deionised H 2 O Adjust pH to 8.0, otherwise the EDTA won't go into solution 500 mM NaEDTA 100 ml 18.6 g EDTA disodium salt dihydrate up to 100 ml deionised H2O Autoclave stock solutions at 121°C
%s %s Extraction Buffer 100 ml 2M KCl 50 ml 1M Tris-HCl pH 8.0 10 ml 500 mM NaEDTA 2 ml deionised H2O up to 100 ml
Per sample prepare 2 x 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes . Label them with sample number and add: Tube 1: 500 ul Extraction Buffer + 1 stainless steel bead Tube 2: 300 ul Isopropanol

With a sterile toothpick or pipette tip remove a fungal colony or a piece of mycelia of about 3-5 mm x 3-5 mm in size from a plate and put it into Tube 1 (containing the Extraction Buffer and steel bead). Try not to use a piece that is too big, as that might lead to carryover of too many PCR inhibitors. I usually use my toothpick like a sewing machine needle, punching out a piece of mycelium all the way to the bottom of the agar plate. Then I use the toothpick to slice underneath the actual mycelial mat to try to cut off as much of the agar as possible.
Homogenise samples for 5 min at 1500 rpm in the MiniG 1600. (SPEX, SP 1600, MiniG 1600 Automated Tissue Homogenizer and Cell Lyser, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/5myft4w.jpg )
Pellet cell debris by centrifugation at 17.000 x g for 5 min .
Transfer supernatant to Tube 2 (Containing 300 ul Isopropanol) Vortex for 5 - 10 seconds Pellet DNA by centrifugation for 5 min at 17.000 x g
Discard supernatant Add 800 ul 70% Ethanol Vortex mix sample, make sure pellet comes loose from bottom of the tube Pellet DNA by centrifugation for 5 min at 17.000 x g

Discard supernatant Remove as much of the ethanol as possible by drawing drops out with a pipette tip Let leftover ethanol evaporate for 5-10 min Dissolve pellet in 50-100 ul H 2 O or 1x TE buffer Measure DNA concentration (e.g. Nanodrop) store DNA at -20°C
creator: Johannes Wolfram JWD Debler
